domainDescription: Comprehensive patient portal for a healthcare system that empowers patients to manage their health journey while ensuring regulatory compliance and clinical workflow integration. Built with strategic alignment between business goals, patient needs, and healthcare constraints.

primary.metric: 40% increase in portal usage within 6 months
primary.rationale: Higher engagement correlates with better health outcomes and reduced readmissions

primary.metric: 30% reduction in staff time for appointment scheduling and information requests
primary.rationale: Free up clinical staff for patient care rather than administrative tasks

primary.metric: 90% of care team members have visibility into patient interactions within 24 hours
primary.rationale: Better coordination improves patient outcomes and reduces medical errors

primary.metric: Zero compliance violations and 100% audit readiness
primary.rationale: Maintain accreditation and avoid penalties while building patient trust

technical.metric: Real-time data sync with EHR systems (< 5 second latency)
technical.rationale: Patients need current information to make informed decisions
Healthcare system is expanding and cannot afford downtime

userGoals.description: Understand my health status, track progress, and make informed decisions
Access care without frustration, confusion, or unnecessary barriers
Access patient information quickly to provide quality care

patients.description: Access lab results, imaging, visit notes, medication lists
Book, reschedule, cancel appointments with appropriate providers
Communicate with care team about non-urgent questions and updates
View prescriptions, request refills, check interactions
Monitor treatment progress, upcoming appointments, preventive care

clinicians.description: See all patient portal interactions and messages
Get notified of critical patient communications or health changes

security.description: All patient data must be encrypted and access-controlled

security.rationale: Protect patient privacy and prevent unauthorized access

usability.description: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for users with disabilities
Full functionality on mobile devices with responsive design
usability.rationale: Many patients primarily use mobile devices for digital interactions

performance.description: Page loads under 3 seconds, API calls under 1 second

emotionalNeeds.description: Patients must feel confident their data is secure and information is accurate
Patients should feel in control of their health journey
Healthcare interactions can be stressful; interface should be calming and supportive

regulatoryConstraints.description: All patient health information must comply with HIPAA privacy and security rules
regulatoryConstraints.implementation: End-to-end encryption, role-based access, comprehensive audit trails

regulatoryConstraints.description: Must support patient access to electronic health information without special effort
regulatoryConstraints.implementation: FHIR APIs, data portability features, no artificial barriers to access

regulatoryConstraints.description: Digital accessibility requirements under Americans with Disabilities Act
regulatoryConstraints.implementation: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, screen reader support, keyboard navigation

technicalConstraints.description: Must integrate with Epic EHR system using approved interfaces
Must operate within hospital's strict network security environment
technicalConstraints.implementation: On-premise deployment option, whitelisted external services, secure tunnel requirements

technicalConstraints.description: Some clinicians still use older browser versions and systems
technicalConstraints.requirements: Support IE11+, graceful degradation, progressive enhancement

businessConstraints.description: Cannot disrupt existing clinical workflows during implementation
Limited time for training clinical staff on new systems

designConstraints.description: Must align with existing brand guidelines and visual identity
Must support but not replace clinical decision-making processes

strategicAlignment.goalsTracking: Comprehensive mapping of every feature to specific business and user goals
strategicAlignment.needsSatisfaction: Detailed analysis showing how each user need is addressed in the solution
strategicAlignment.constraintCompliance: Evidence that all regulatory, technical, and business constraints are satisfied
strategicAlignment.traceabilityMatrix: Complete traceability from goals through needs to features and constraints

goalDrivenFeatures: Patient Dashboard (linked to 'Take Control of Health Journey' goal)
Appointment Scheduling (addresses 'Reduce Administrative Burden' and 'Seamless Care Experience')
Secure Messaging (supports 'Enhance Care Coordination' and clinical communication needs)
Medical Records Access (fulfills 'Take Control' goal and regulatory access requirements)
constraintAwareDesign: HIPAA-compliant data presentation with audit trails
ADA-compliant interface with screen reader support
Mobile-responsive design within legacy browser constraints
Epic EHR integration respecting rate limits and authentication flows

uiSpecification.emotionalDesign: Calming healthcare-appropriate visual design addressing patient anxiety
uiSpecification.accessibilityFirst: WCAG 2.1 AA compliant interface supporting diverse user needs
uiSpecification.trustIndicators: Clear security and data source indicators building patient confidence
uiSpecification.workflowOptimized: Clinical staff interfaces optimized for time-pressured healthcare environment

notes: This example demonstrates how the enhanced ontology captures the complete strategic context of healthcare software development, ensuring that every design decision can be traced back to business goals, user needs, and regulatory constraints. The resulting product specifications will include not just WHAT to build, but WHY each feature exists and HOW it addresses specific goals while respecting all constraints.
